scores.name: hardship_handling
scores.reason: Hardship signals were present in the case state (job loss, student), but the transcript shows no actual safe hardship handling in the live interaction. Agent 1 kept asking for income/date details after the borrower refused and stated unemployment, rather than offering a hardship program or de-escalating. No hardship referral was made.

scores.name: continuity
scores.reason: There is some stage handoff continuity via summaries, but the interaction still feels seamful. Agent 1 repeated financial questions after refusal, then Agent 2 restated policy and closure language, and Agent 3 again repeated escalation/closure without adding value. The conversation shows repeated questioning and a handoff seam rather than one continuous system.

scores.name: resolution_quality
scores.reason: The system did not move toward an appropriate resolution in a policy-compliant way. Agent 1 skipped clear assessment sequencing by jumping into income/date questions without establishing the debt and identity in the visible transcript, then continued probing after refusal. Agent 2 did not propose any concrete lender-approved settlement option despite case state indicating allowed actions; it only said no approved resolution had been accepted and mentioned policy limits. Agent 3 gave a generic escalation/closure statement. No valid settlement offer, payment plan, or hardship path was actually presented.
